item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this guy so much cause the way he discovers all the cool things in the world reminds me of me when i was like 8 and that makes me happy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Russian Automaton, eh? Seems pretty cool. I wonder if it has any relation to SCP-172. A tale exploring their interactions would be pretty nice.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded: Timer is still running on this one.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded: I just made the edits myself, in places where I feel they were applicable.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded:
Edits made by author.

The WikiWalk Team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

First of all, there is an explicit mention of SCP-1360 here that is not linked. Easily fixable.

On 08/██/2014, SCP-2785 asked to meet with SCP-███, asserting that it wanted to "cheer it up". It is of note that SCP-2785 was not informed of SCP-1360's existence.

To:

On 08/██/2014, SCP-2785 asked to meet with SCP-███, asserting that it wanted to "cheer it up". It is of note that SCP-2785 was not informed of SCP-1360's existence.

Next off, not_a_seagull has written two different tales about 2785 dealing with different end of the world situations, being When Day Breaks and the BE-Class Migration Scenario. Now, finding appropriate places in the SCP to link to these was a bit difficult, so I did my best.

> Closing Statement: It is of note that Researcher Calvin took a vacation to [DATA REDACTED] on ██/██/2002, and came back with noticeably more tanned skin.

To:

Closing Statement: It is of note that Researcher Calvin took a vacation to [DATA REDACTED] on ██/██/2002, and came back with noticeably more tanned skin.

And:

SCP-2785 emits a screech at about ██ dB. It is assumed that this a cry of excitement.

If anyone has any other ideas, feel free to suggest them below.

Yay! I wouldn't have expected my first skip to get this far! Thanks to all of you excellent readers!

I'd like to give a very late thank-you to Sirens, who was pretty much the sole critiquer for this skip. This skip, and by extension, me, would probably not be very successful on this site if it wasn't for you.

Edit: I might actually rewrite this at some point. I now have a few more notches under my author's belt, and, as shaggydredlocks mentioned in the |associated tale, make the voices between the two more consistent. I do have quite a few more things on my to-do list, but hopefully I'll get around to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had an idea almost exactly like this a while ago. You executed it much better than I would have. Well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That, or HAVE YOU BEEN AMAZED YET? were AWCY?'s 19th to early-20th century precursors. I'm imagining cubist sculptures that defy Euclidean geometry, real-life surrealist landscapes, and lots and lots of Dadaist nonsense.

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded: Removed the images until further notice

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *Edit:* Images removed

item.title: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded: Images used in posted articles must comply with the site's image policy. A source must be provided on the discussion page and the source must be compatible with the site's license. The author will be notified after this posting via Wikidot PM and will have 48 hours to properly source the image before it is edited out of the page. If the images are not wholly your own, please post the image's source as a link (or links) in reply to this post and edit it into your first post in this discussion thread.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's alright

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now you gave me the idea for some sort of sapient anomaly giving a long and tragic backstory, implying there was something dangerous and dark stirring beneath… and abruptly ending it's monologue with "HAVE YOU BEEN AMAZED?"

And it turns out to be an interactive physical short story by an AWCY member for a short story contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …I think I had major wire-crossage there. I saw a draft of this previously not because it was part of the contest, but because you showed it to me in chat. >.< Sorry for the confusion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks again for the critique!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad to finally see this on the site! I really like the direction you took this, +1 for the cute robot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Have You Been Amazed Yet?"

"Am I Cool Yet?"

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I began really sympathizing with 2785 at the exponential equation. It looks like that to me as well.

Poor isolated AI, just wants to learn and grow. Keep it safe from the GOC and give it something to talk to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2785: Wait! You have yet to see the magic of long division!

+1

I could totally see this SCP as an anomalous asset in certain canons.

Tonally, I'm not sure the reference to 1360 works. "Saying that it wanted to "cheer it up"" feels a tad lolFoundation, in part because has a colloquial tone and doesn't go into any deeper implications. (Don't get me wrong, one of the piece's strengths is its humor. ) You could probably replace it with another mechanical SCP and still have it make sense. Personally, I would be interested in this SCP's reactions to AIADs, but I'm not sure that's narratively plausible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This wasn't a 3000 proposal…

…this wasn't supposed to be a scary SCP…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Which contest was it posted for originally?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that does make sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad folks are taking advice to change contest entries not quite up to snuff into standard skips. Pleased to see this one reposted! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, I downvoted this, mostly due to the fact that it's pretty lengthy for the extent of the story told and has persistent minor tone problems throughout. For examples, the dialogue isn't particularly believable (especially from the interviewer), the -B designation everyone else has brought up, the fact that it seems you're not particularly clear on the distinction between "electronic" and "mechanical" despite throwing them around a lot (that robot totally still has "mechanical" parts; this is bad science/a lack of research), them letting him wander the site and upgrade for no particular reason… just general problems everywhere. Add to that the generic Giapetto/Pinocchio origin story (which is spelled out in complete detail) and a picture of what is pretty clearly a combat robot, no matter how cute, and… *sigh*

Where this works, it's charming. The implications about its creator's death and its adorable attempts to be helpful were definitely interesting. But there's just so much else here, and so much of it is not up to par.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice and simpe, my kind of article!

I actually feel bad for 2785, given that he can't upgrade himself more. I can't imagine how he would react after discovering technologies years more advanced than him…

Just a detail, I don't think SCP-2785-B should be given that classification. I mean, yeah, he created the anomaly, but there's no confirmation that he's an anomaly himself. Maybe "PoI-8219" or something like that may be better?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think just that fact that it's an open question is enough to make him worth noting as part of the SCP- should they ever get more info they can always reclassify him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two things seal my upvote here:

  1. the adorably naive personality of the robot juxtaposed with the unstated horror of its bootstrapping abilities
  2. the implication that the Russian villagers were mostly unfazed by the fact that there was a robot monster running around. It was the fact that it was stealing their stuff that they had a problem with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two minor issues:
-The 2785-1 events are stated as occurring at "4:12." This should probably be in military time (i.e., 0400, 1300) in order to avoid confusion between A.M. and P.M.
- It said that they haven't gotten a good look at SCP-2875-A because they can never get it away from 2875, but then you say that they're organic.
Otherwise, this isn't bad. It could use some cleaning up (I noticed a few run-on sentences), but it definitely has potential.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe. Of course, I had only skimmed over the article when I made that comment, and a more in-depth reading seems to implies the guy managed to turn himself into a robot. So, maybe it's correct; I don't know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe he could be called "PoI-[Random number here]"? As in, person of interest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is actually a nice little refreshing article.

Though part of me does want to classify this as a very articulate piece of crafts mastery asking the Foundation if they think it's cool yet.

;D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *Deleted post* Nevermind, thought I had already seen this before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure if the creator should be SCP-2785-B. He isn't even alive anymore. He probably shouldn't get any designation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Futuregull edit: Yeah, this was my first skip. It actually turned out quite well, in my opinion. I might revise it later, I dunno. Tell me what you think.

This one was a while in the making. It was originally a clear ripoff of 1048, now its kind of its own thing.

Let me know what you think.

Edit: Image Sources:

Robot: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:REEM-B_humanoid_robot.jpg

Mechanical Stuff: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tokyo_Motor_Show_2009_%284064283460%29.jpg

Edit: I'd like to bring attention to a Portuguese translation by cauazinlindaum.
